top_stories.title: Microsoft Makes Passkeys Default: What Identity And Security Leaders Need To Do
top_stories.summary: Microsoft announced that passkeys will become the default authentication method for all Microsoft Entra ID users starting September 1, 2026. The change will automatically enable passkey authentication, marking a significant shift in phishing-resistant MFA adoption across the platform.

top_stories.title: Microsoft is forcing an enterprise transition to passkeys
top_stories.summary: Microsoft Entra ID will replace SMS and voice authentication with passkeys starting in September, forcing enterprise users to transition away from traditional credential methods. The change is driven by concerns over AI-powered attacks making legacy authentication factors increasingly vulnerable.

top_stories.title: Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause
top_stories.summary: Identity-based attacks have surpassed exploits as the leading root cause of ransomware incidents. MFA was present in 97% of credential-based attacks yet failed to prevent compromise, highlighting critical gaps in authentication defenses.

top_stories.title: Claude can now sign into websites with 1Password without exposing your credentials
top_stories.summary: 1Password has launched a beta integration called "1Password for Claude" that enables Anthropic's Claude AI assistant to authenticate to websites during browser-based tasks without exposing user credentials or secrets. The integration works across individual, family, and business 1Password plans and requires Claude Desktop on macOS, Claude in Chrome, and the 1Password desktop app with browser extension.

top_stories.title: Oak raised $60M to give every user, machine, and AI agent one identity system
top_stories.summary: Oak, an Israeli IAM startup, emerged from stealth with a $60M seed round to build a unified identity system covering human users, machines, and AI agents. The company is targeting the growing challenge of managing non-human identities alongside traditional user identities in enterprise environments.

news.title: OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
news.summary: Okta's Red Team discovered and reported a denial-of-service vulnerability in OpenSSL, dubbed "HollowByte," which allows an 11-byte TLS request to cause an unpatched server to reserve up to 131 KB of memory indefinitely. OpenSSL shipped a fix in June without a CVE, advisory, or changelog entry, and Okta published the research detailing the flaw's behavior on glibc systems.

news.title: The script, not the voice, is what makes AI voice phishing work
news.summary: Researchers from Harvard Kennedy School, Meta, and others conducted a study with 4,100 US adults testing AI voice phishing using six commercial voice systems and human callers. The study found that the script content, not the voice quality, is the primary factor in successful voice phishing attacks targeting password resets and credential access.

news.title: New NadMesh Botnet Hunts Exposed AI Services for Cloud Keys and Kubernetes Tokens
news.summary: A Go-based botnet called NadMesh has been discovered targeting exposed AI services to harvest cloud credentials, with the operator's dashboard claiming 3,811 unique AWS keys compromised. The botnet scans for misconfigured AI tools including ComfyUI, Ollama, and Langflow using Shodan to harvest AWS keys and Kubernetes tokens.

news.title: ACR Stealer Uses ClickFix Lures to Steal Browser Tokens and Microsoft 365 Files
news.summary: ACR Stealer, an infostealer active since 2024, is stealing browser passwords, session tokens, and Microsoft 365 documents including files from OneDrive and SharePoint. The malware uses ClickFix social engineering techniques, tricking users into pasting malicious commands into a Run box, as detailed by Microsoft's Defender Experts team.

news.title: AI Agents Broke the Security Playbook. Here's What Replaces It.
news.summary: Token Security published a piece arguing that AI agents require a new approach to identity security, moving away from traditional security workflows toward a live identity foundation. The article frames AI agents as operating at a speed and scale that breaks conventional IAM workflows, positioning dynamic identity infrastructure as the replacement model.

news.title: Two Scattered Spider Hackers Get 5.5 Years Each for £29 Million TfL Hack
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider hacking group were sentenced to 5.5 years each for the 2024 hack of Transport for London, which compromised 148 systems and forced all 27,000 employees to reset passwords in person. The attack is notable from an IAM perspective for the large-scale credential reset operation it triggered across the organization.

news.title: n8n Token Exchange Flaw Could Let Attackers Log In as Users From Another Issuer
news.summary: A security flaw in n8n's workflow automation platform allowed attackers to log in as other users by exploiting improper JWT validation on Enterprise instances configured with multiple token issuers. The vulnerability matched incoming JWTs on the `sub` claim while ignoring the `iss` claim, meaning a valid token from one issuer could authenticate as a user registered under a different issuer.

news.title: Zoom Patches Critical Windows Flaw That Could Enable Account Takeover
news.summary: Zoom has patched a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-53412, CVSS 9.8) in Zoom Workplace for Windows that could enable account takeover. The flaw, caused by improper input validation, affects versions before 7.0.0 and multiple VDI Client branches, and has been addressed in the latest security updates.

news.title: Building Secure APIs with OAuth 2.1 and OpenID Connect
news.summary: The article covers API security implementation using OAuth 2.1 and OpenID Connect, addressing authentication and authorization flows, token management, PKCE, and best practices. It targets developers and security professionals working with modern identity protocols and standards.

news.title: ESDS Launches Swaraj Nandi and Swaraj Hansa, Two Sovereign Enterprise Security Platforms Built Entirely in India - The Tribune
news.summary: ESDS launched two enterprise security platforms, Swaraj Nandi (focused on privileged access management) and Swaraj Hansa (focused on security alert monitoring), built in India as sovereign solutions. Swaraj Nandi targets privileged access governance, positioning it as a PAM and identity governance offering for enterprise customers.

news.title: Least privilege for AI agents: Identity, access, and tool binding
news.summary: Microsoft published a blog post on applying least privilege principles to AI agents, covering identity, access controls, and tool binding. The article addresses how to implement IAM concepts like strong identity verification, access management, and auditing for autonomous AI agents.

news.title: Keeper Security Closes the Standing Privilege Gap in Cloud Identity with Keeper Privileged Cloud
news.summary: Keeper Security has launched Keeper Privileged Cloud, a new just-in-time access capability within its KeeperPAM platform. The feature ensures privileged credentials are granted only when needed and automatically revoked upon session expiration, addressing standing privilege risks in cloud identity environments.

news.title: Microsoft introduces registry policy to automate Windows SSO consent
news.summary: Microsoft has introduced a registry-based control enabling IT administrators to automatically bypass SSO permission prompts on managed Windows 11 devices. The feature targets enterprise environments running specific Windows 11 versions, allowing automated consent management for single sign-on workflows.

news.title: Microsoft makes Windows SSO prompts easier to manage
news.summary: Microsoft is introducing a new registry-based policy that allows IT administrators to automatically accept Windows SSO permissions on managed Windows 11 devices (versions 24H2 and 25H2). The feature applies to devices managed with Microsoft Entra ID, giving admins greater control over SSO prompts for enterprise-managed environments.

news.title: NIMC, IT firms push for secure digital government services
news.summary: Nigeria's National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) and IT firms are advocating for secure digital government services with an emphasis on cybersecurity and digital identity. The initiative focuses on improving public services through stronger digital identity frameworks in Nigeria.

news.title: Delinea Launches New Partner Program to Drive Partner Profitability and Scale Growth
news.summary: Delinea has launched a new partner program called the Delinea Partner Advantage Program. The program is designed to offer identity security technology, protected discounts, and streamlined business processes for channel partners.

news.title: Microsoft’s July 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes 622 flaws and 2 exploited zero-days
news.summary: Microsoft's July 2026 Patch Tuesday addresses 622 CVEs, including actively exploited zero-days in Active Directory Federation Services (AD FS) and SharePoint. The update also includes a fix for a disclosed BitLocker bypass vulnerability, all requiring urgent patching action from administrators.

news.title: Oak Raises $60M in Seed Funding to Build the AI-Native Identity Operating System
news.summary: Oak, founded by Shai Morag and Tal Marom, has raised $60M in seed funding to build an AI-native identity operating system. The platform aims to replace legacy identity architecture by governing both human and AI agent identities in a unified system.

news.title: SailPoint、パートナーのイノベーションを促進する「ユニファイド プラットフォーム アクセス」プログラムを発表
news.summary: SailPoint announced a new "Unified Platform Access" program aimed at accelerating partner innovation, as announced in the US on June 16, 2026. The program provides partners with access to SailPoint's unified platform, with this release being the Japanese translation of the original US press release.

news.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
news.summary: CISA released a postmortem report on a data breach in which a contractor accidentally published internal CISA credentials, including AWS GovCloud keys, to a public GitHub repository for nearly six months. The incident highlights credential management failures and was only flagged after KrebsOnSecurity notified the agency.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used to compromise organizational accounts.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize accounts, including the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official's profiles. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to bypass normal authentication and force password resets were circulated on Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.
